steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Identify corresponding sides from the similarity statement
steps.content:
Since △QPR ∼ △STR, the vertices correspond in order: Q↔S, P↔T, R↔R. Therefore, the corresponding sides are:
  • $QP$ ↔ $ST$
  • $PR$ ↔ $TR$
  • $QR$ ↔ $SR$ (the hypotenuses)

steps.title: Set up a proportion and solve for SR
steps.content:
From the similarity, all pairs of corresponding sides have the same ratio:
$$\frac{QR}{SR} = \frac{QP}{ST}$$
Substitute the given lengths:
$$\frac{25}{SR} = \frac{15}{14}$$
Cross-multiply:
$$15 \cdot SR = 25 \cdot 14$$
$$SR = \frac{350}{15}$$
A is correct.
